implication: Both N- and C-domain positions are held by neighboring subunits, forming inter-subunit N-domain and C-domain rings. A single subunit (monomer) cannot recover the native inter-domain arrangement. Position-addressability designs MUST be evaluated in >=dimer context. This is why the cycle-1 native-order 2-mer reformed R146 while monomers do not, and it directly motivates the dimer-first (B1) strategy.
